verses.bsb: Not only that, but we ourselves, who have the firstfruits of the Spirit, groan inwardly as we wait eagerly for our adoption as sons, the redemption of our bodies.

verses.esv: For in this hope we were saved. Now hope that is seen is not hope. For who hopes for what he sees?
verses.kjv: For we are saved by hope: but hope that is seen is not hope: for what a man seeth, why doth he yet hope for?
verses.bsb: For in this hope we were saved; but hope that is seen is no hope at all. Who hopes for what he can already see?

verses.esv: But if we hope for what we do not see, we wait for it with patience.
verses.kjv: But if we hope for that we see not, then do we with patience wait for it.
verses.bsb: But if we hope for what we do not yet see, we wait for it patiently.

verses.esv: Likewise the Spirit helps us in our weakness. For we do not know what to pray for as we ought, but the Spirit himself intercedes for us with groanings too deep for words.
verses.kjv: Likewise the Spirit also helpeth our infirmities: for we know not what we should pray for as we ought: but the Spirit itself maketh intercession for us with groanings which cannot be uttered.
verses.bsb: In the same way, the Spirit helps us in our weakness. For we do not know how we ought to pray, but the Spirit Himself intercedes for us with groans too deep for words.

verses.esv: And he who searches hearts knows what is the mind of the Spirit, because the Spirit intercedes for the saints according to the will of God.
verses.kjv: And he that searcheth the hearts knoweth what is the mind of the Spirit, because he maketh intercession for the saints according to the will of God.
verses.bsb: And He who searches our hearts knows the mind of the Spirit, because the Spirit intercedes for the saints according to the will of God.

verses.esv: And we know that for those who love God all things work together for good, for those who are called according to his purpose.
verses.kjv: And we know that all things work together for good to them that love God, to them who are the called according to his purpose.
verses.bsb: And we know that God works all things together for the good of those who love Him, who are called according to His purpose.

verses.esv: For those whom he foreknew he also predestined to be conformed to the image of his Son, in order that he might be the firstborn among many brothers.
verses.kjv: For whom he did foreknow, he also did predestinate to be conformed to the image of his Son, that he might be the firstborn among many brethren.
verses.bsb: For those God foreknew, He also predestined to be conformed to the image of His Son, so that He would be the firstborn among many brothers.

verses.esv: And those whom he predestined he also called, and those whom he called he also justified, and those whom he justified he also glorified.
verses.kjv: Moreover whom he did predestinate, them he also called: and whom he called, them he also justified: and whom he justified, them he also glorified.
verses.bsb: And those He predestined, He also called; those He called, He also justified; those He justified, He also glorified.

verses.esv: What then shall we say to these things? If God is for us, who can be against us?
verses.kjv: What shall we then say to these things? If God be for us, who can be against us?
verses.bsb: What then shall we say in response to these things? If God is for us, who can be against us?

verses.esv: He who did not spare his own Son but gave him up for us all, how will he not also with him graciously give us all things?
verses.kjv: He that spared not his own Son, but delivered him up for us all, how shall he not with him also freely give us all things?
verses.bsb: He who did not spare His own Son but gave Him up for us all, how will He not also, along with Him, freely give us all things?

verses.esv: Who shall bring any charge against God's elect? It is God who justifies.
verses.kjv: Who shall lay any thing to the charge of God’s elect? It is God that justifieth.
verses.bsb: Who will bring any charge against God’s elect? It is God who justifies.

verses.esv: Who is to condemn? Christ Jesus is the one who died—more than that, who was raised—who is at the right hand of God, who indeed is interceding for us.
verses.kjv: Who is he that condemneth? It is Christ that died, yea rather, that is risen again, who is even at the right hand of God, who also maketh intercession for us.
verses.bsb: Who is there to condemn us? For Christ Jesus, who died, and more than that was raised to life, is at the right hand of God—and He is interceding for us.

verses.esv: Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? Shall tribulation, or distress, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or danger, or sword?
verses.kjv: Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? shall tribulation, or distress, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or peril, or sword?
verses.bsb: Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? Shall trouble or distress or persecution or famine or nakedness or danger or sword?

verses.esv: As it is written, “For your sake we are being killed all the day long; we are regarded as sheep to be slaughtered.”
verses.kjv: As it is written, For thy sake we are killed all the day long; we are accounted as sheep for the slaughter.
verses.bsb: As it is written:  “For Your sake we face death all day long; we are considered as sheep to be slaughtered.”

verses.esv: No, in all these things we are more than conquerors through him who loved us.
verses.kjv: Nay, in all these things we are more than conquerors through him that loved us.
verses.bsb: No, in all these things we are more than conquerors through Him who loved us.

verses.esv: For I am sure that neither death nor life, nor angels nor rulers, nor things present nor things to come, nor powers,
verses.kjv: For I am persuaded, that neither death, nor life, nor angels, nor principalities, nor powers, nor things present, nor things to come,
verses.bsb: For I am convinced that neither death nor life, neither angels nor principalities, neither the present nor the future, nor any powers,

verses.esv: nor height nor depth, nor anything else in all creation, will be able to separate us from the love of God in Christ Jesus our Lord.
verses.kjv: Nor height, nor depth, nor any other creature, shall be able to separate us from the love of God, which is in Christ Jesus our Lord.
verses.bsb: neither height nor depth, nor anything else in all creation, will be able to separate us from the love of God that is in Christ Jesus our Lord.

verses.esv: I am speaking the truth in Christ—I am not lying; my conscience bears me witness in the Holy Spirit—
verses.kjv: I SAY the truth in Christ, I lie not, my conscience also bearing me witness in the Holy Ghost,
verses.bsb: I speak the truth in Christ; I am not lying, as confirmed by my conscience in the Holy Spirit.

verses.esv: that I have great sorrow and unceasing anguish in my heart.
verses.kjv: That I have great heaviness and continual sorrow in my heart.
verses.bsb: I have deep sorrow and unceasing anguish in my heart.

verses.esv: For I could wish that I myself were accursed and cut off from Christ for the sake of my brothers, my kinsmen according to the flesh.
verses.kjv: For I could wish that myself were accursed from Christ for my brethren, my kinsmen according to the flesh:
verses.bsb: For I could wish that I myself were cursed and cut off from Christ for the sake of my brothers, my own flesh and blood,

verses.esv: They are Israelites, and to them belong the adoption, the glory, the covenants, the giving of the law, the worship, and the promises.
verses.kjv: Who are Israelites; to whom pertaineth the adoption, and the glory, and the covenants, and the giving of the law, and the service of God, and the promises;
verses.bsb: the people of Israel. Theirs is the adoption as sons; theirs the divine glory and the covenants; theirs the giving of the law, the temple worship, and the promises.

verses.esv: To them belong the patriarchs, and from their race, according to the flesh, is the Christ, who is God over all, blessed forever. Amen.
verses.kjv: Whose are the fathers, and of whom as concerning the flesh Christ came, who is over all, God blessed for ever. Amen.
verses.bsb: Theirs are the patriarchs, and from them proceeds the human descent of Christ, who is God over all, forever worthy of praise! Amen.

verses.esv: But it is not as though the word of God has failed. For not all who are descended from Israel belong to Israel,
verses.kjv: Not as though the word of God hath taken none effect. For they are not all Israel, which are of Israel:
verses.bsb: It is not as though God’s word has failed. For not all who are descended from Israel are Israel.

verses.esv: and not all are children of Abraham because they are his offspring, but “Through Isaac shall your offspring be named.”
verses.kjv: Neither, because they are the seed of Abraham, are they all children: but, In Isaac shall thy seed be called.
verses.bsb: Nor because they are Abraham’s descendants are they all his children. On the contrary, “Through Isaac your offspring will be reckoned.”

verses.esv: This means that it is not the children of the flesh who are the children of God, but the children of the promise are counted as offspring.
verses.kjv: That is, They which are the children of the flesh, these are not the children of God: but the children of the promise are counted for the seed.
verses.bsb: So it is not the children of the flesh who are God’s children, but it is the children of the promise who are regarded as offspring.

verses.esv: For this is what the promise said: “About this time next year I will return, and Sarah shall have a son.”
verses.kjv: For this is the word of promise, At this time will I come, and Sara shall have a son.
verses.bsb: For this is what the promise stated: “At the appointed time I will return, and Sarah will have a son.”

verses.esv: And not only so, but also when Rebekah had conceived children by one man, our forefather Isaac,
verses.kjv: And not only this; but when Rebecca also had conceived by one, even by our father Isaac;
verses.bsb: Not only that, but Rebecca’s children were conceived by one man, our father Isaac.

verses.esv: though they were not yet born and had done nothing either good or bad—in order that God's purpose of election might continue, not because of works but because of him who calls—
verses.kjv: (For the children being not yet born, neither having done any good or evil, that the purpose of God according to election might stand, not of works, but of him that calleth;)
verses.bsb: Yet before the twins were born or had done anything good or bad, in order that God’s plan of election might stand,

verses.esv: she was told, “The older will serve the younger.”
verses.kjv: It was said unto her, The elder shall serve the younger.
verses.bsb: not by works but by Him who calls, she was told, “The older will serve the younger.”

verses.esv: As it is written, “Jacob I loved, but Esau I hated.”
verses.kjv: As it is written, Jacob have I loved, but Esau have I hated.
verses.bsb: So it is written: “Jacob I loved, but Esau I hated.”

verses.esv: What shall we say then? Is there injustice on God's part? By no means!
verses.kjv: What shall we say then? Is there unrighteousness with God? God forbid.
verses.bsb: What then shall we say? Is God unjust? Certainly not!

verses.esv: For he says to Moses, “I will have mercy on whom I have mercy, and I will have compassion on whom I have compassion.”
verses.kjv: For he saith to Moses, I will have mercy on whom I will have mercy, and I will have compassion on whom I will have compassion.
verses.bsb: For He says to Moses:  “I will have mercy on whom I have mercy, and I will have compassion on whom I have compassion.”

verses.esv: So then it depends not on human will or exertion, but on God, who has mercy.
verses.kjv: So then it is not of him that willeth, nor of him that runneth, but of God that sheweth mercy.
verses.bsb: So then, it does not depend on man’s desire or effort, but on God’s mercy.

verses.esv: For the Scripture says to Pharaoh, “For this very purpose I have raised you up, that I might show my power in you, and that my name might be proclaimed in all the earth.”
verses.kjv: For the scripture saith unto Pharaoh, Even for this same purpose have I raised thee up, that I might shew my power in thee, and that my name might be declared throughout all the earth.
verses.bsb: For the Scripture says to Pharaoh: “I raised you up for this very purpose, that I might display My power in you, and that My name might be proclaimed in all the earth.”

verses.esv: So then he has mercy on whomever he wills, and he hardens whomever he wills.
verses.kjv: Therefore hath he mercy on whom he will have mercy, and whom he will he hardeneth.
verses.bsb: Therefore God has mercy on whom He wants to have mercy, and He hardens whom He wants to harden.

verses.esv: You will say to me then, “Why does he still find fault? For who can resist his will?”
verses.kjv: Thou wilt say then unto me, Why doth he yet find fault? For who hath resisted his will?
verses.bsb: One of you will say to me, “Then why does God still find fault? For who can resist His will?”

verses.esv: But who are you, O man, to answer back to God? Will what is molded say to its molder, “Why have you made me like this?”
verses.kjv: Nay but, O man, who art thou that repliest against God? Shall the thing formed say to him that formed it, Why hast thou made me thus?
verses.bsb: But who are you, O man, to talk back to God? Shall what is formed say to Him who formed it, “Why did You make me like this?”

verses.esv: Has the potter no right over the clay, to make out of the same lump one vessel for honorable use and another for dishonorable use?
verses.kjv: Hath not the potter power over the clay, of the same lump to make one vessel unto honour, and another unto dishonour?
verses.bsb: Does not the potter have the right to make from the same lump of clay one vessel for special occasions and another for common use?

verses.esv: What if God, desiring to show his wrath and to make known his power, has endured with much patience vessels of wrath prepared for destruction,
verses.kjv: What if God, willing to shew his wrath, and to make his power known, endured with much longsuffering the vessels of wrath fitted to destruction:
verses.bsb: What if God, intending to show His wrath and make His power known, bore with great patience the vessels of His wrath, prepared for destruction?

verses.esv: in order to make known the riches of his glory for vessels of mercy, which he has prepared beforehand for glory—
verses.kjv: And that he might make known the riches of his glory on the vessels of mercy, which he had afore prepared unto glory,
verses.bsb: What if He did this to make the riches of His glory known to the vessels of His mercy, whom He prepared in advance for glory—

verses.esv: even us whom he has called, not from the Jews only but also from the Gentiles?
verses.kjv: Even us, whom he hath called, not of the Jews only, but also of the Gentiles?
verses.bsb: including us, whom He has called not only from the Jews, but also from the Gentiles?

verses.esv: As indeed he says in Hosea, “Those who were not my people I will call ‘my people,’ and her who was not beloved I will call ‘beloved.’”
verses.kjv: As he saith also in Osee, I will call them my people, which were not my people; and her beloved, which was not beloved.
verses.bsb: As He says in Hosea:  “I will call them ‘My People’ who are not My people, and I will call her ‘My Beloved’ who is not My beloved,”

verses.esv: “And in the very place where it was said to them, ‘You are not my people,’ there they will be called ‘sons of the living God.’”
verses.kjv: And it shall come to pass, that in the place where it was said unto them, Ye are not my people; there shall they be called the children of the living God.
verses.bsb: and,  “It will happen that in the very place where it was said to them, ‘You are not My people,’ they will be called ‘sons of the living God.’”

verses.esv: And Isaiah cries out concerning Israel: “Though the number of the sons of Israel be as the sand of the sea, only a remnant of them will be saved,
verses.kjv: Esaias also crieth concerning Israel, Though the number of the children of Israel be as the sand of the sea, a remnant shall be saved:
verses.bsb: Isaiah cries out concerning Israel:  “Though the number of the Israelites is like the sand of the sea, only the remnant will be saved.

verses.esv: for the Lord will carry out his sentence upon the earth fully and without delay.”
verses.kjv: For he will finish the work, and cut it short in righteousness: because a short work will the Lord make upon the earth.
verses.bsb: For the Lord will carry out His sentence on the earth thoroughly and decisively.”

verses.esv: And as Isaiah predicted, “If the Lord of hosts had not left us offspring, we would have been like Sodom and become like Gomorrah.”
verses.kjv: And as Esaias said before, Except the Lord of Sabaoth had left us a seed, we had been as Sodoma, and been made like unto Gomorrha.
verses.bsb: It is just as Isaiah foretold:  “Unless the Lord of Hosts had left us descendants, we would have become like Sodom, we would have resembled Gomorrah.”

verses.esv: What shall we say, then? That Gentiles who did not pursue righteousness have attained it, that is, a righteousness that is by faith;
verses.kjv: What shall we say then? That the Gentiles, which followed not after righteousness, have attained to righteousness, even the righteousness which is of faith.
verses.bsb: What then will we say? That the Gentiles, who did not pursue righteousness, have obtained it, a righteousness that is by faith;

verses.esv: but that Israel who pursued a law that would lead to righteousness did not succeed in reaching that law.
verses.kjv: But Israel, which followed after the law of righteousness, hath not attained to the law of righteousness.
verses.bsb: but Israel, who pursued a law of righteousness, has not attained it.

verses.esv: Why? Because they did not pursue it by faith, but as if it were based on works. They have stumbled over the stumbling stone,
verses.kjv: Wherefore? Because they sought it not by faith, but as it were by the works of the law. For they stumbled at that stumblingstone;
verses.bsb: Why not? Because their pursuit was not by faith, but as if it were by works. They stumbled over the stumbling stone,

verses.esv: as it is written, “Behold, I am laying in Zion a stone of stumbling, and a rock of offense; and whoever believes in him will not be put to shame.”
verses.kjv: As it is written, Behold, I lay in Sion a stumblingstone and rock of offence: and whosoever believeth on him shall not be ashamed.
verses.bsb: as it is written:  “See, I lay in Zion a stone of stumbling and a rock of offense; and the one who believes in Him will never be put to shame.”

verses.esv: Brothers, my heart's desire and prayer to God for them is that they may be saved.
verses.kjv: BRETHREN, my heart’s desire and prayer to God for Israel is, that they might be saved.
verses.bsb: Brothers, my heart’s desire and prayer to God for the Israelites is for their salvation.

verses.esv: For I bear them witness that they have a zeal for God, but not according to knowledge.
verses.kjv: For I bear them record that they have a zeal of God, but not according to knowledge.
verses.bsb: For I testify about them that they are zealous for God, but not on the basis of knowledge.

verses.esv: For, being ignorant of the righteousness of God, and seeking to establish their own, they did not submit to God's righteousness.
verses.kjv: For they being ignorant of God’s righteousness, and going about to establish their own righteousness, have not submitted themselves unto the righteousness of God.
verses.bsb: Because they were ignorant of God’s righteousness and sought to establish their own, they did not submit to God’s righteousness.

verses.esv: For Christ is the end of the law for righteousness to everyone who believes.
verses.kjv: For Christ is the end of the law for righteousness to every one that believeth.
verses.bsb: For Christ is the end of the law, to bring righteousness to everyone who believes.

verses.esv: For Moses writes about the righteousness that is based on the law, that the person who does the commandments shall live by them.
verses.kjv: For Moses describeth the righteousness which is of the law, That the man which doeth those things shall live by them.
verses.bsb: For concerning the righteousness that is by the law, Moses writes: “The man who does these things will live by them.”

verses.esv: But the righteousness based on faith says, “Do not say in your heart, ‘Who will ascend into heaven?’” (that is, to bring Christ down)
verses.kjv: But the righteousness which is of faith speaketh on this wise, Say not in thine heart, Who shall ascend into heaven? (that is, to bring Christ down from above:)
verses.bsb: But the righteousness that is by faith says: “Do not say in your heart, ‘Who will ascend into heaven?’ (that is, to bring Christ down)

verses.esv: “or ‘Who will descend into the abyss?’” (that is, to bring Christ up from the dead).
verses.kjv: Or, Who shall descend into the deep? (that is, to bring up Christ again from the dead.)
verses.bsb: or, ‘Who will descend into the Abyss?’ (that is, to bring Christ up from the dead).”

verses.esv: But what does it say? “The word is near you, in your mouth and in your heart” (that is, the word of faith that we proclaim);
verses.kjv: But what saith it? The word is nigh thee, even in thy mouth, and in thy heart: that is, the word of faith, which we preach;
verses.bsb: But what does it say? “The word is near you; it is in your mouth and in your heart,” that is, the word of faith we are proclaiming:

verses.esv: because, if you confess with your mouth that Jesus is Lord and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.
verses.kjv: That if thou shalt confess with thy mouth the Lord Jesus, and shalt believe in thine heart that God hath raised him from the dead, thou shalt be saved.
verses.bsb: that if you confess with your mouth, “Jesus is Lord,” and believe in your heart that God raised Him from the dead, you will be saved.

verses.esv: For with the heart one believes and is justified, and with the mouth one confesses and is saved.
verses.kjv: For with the heart man believeth unto righteousness; and with the mouth confession is made unto salvation.
verses.bsb: For with your heart you believe and are justified, and with your mouth you confess and are saved.

verses.esv: For the Scripture says, “Everyone who believes in him will not be put to shame.”
verses.kjv: For the scripture saith, Whosoever believeth on him shall not be ashamed.
verses.bsb: It is just as the Scripture says: “Anyone who believes in Him will never be put to shame.”

verses.esv: For there is no distinction between Jew and Greek; for the same Lord is Lord of all, bestowing his riches on all who call on him.
verses.kjv: For there is no difference between the Jew and the Greek: for the same Lord over all is rich unto all that call upon him.
verses.bsb: For there is no difference between Jew and Greek: The same Lord is Lord of all, and gives richly to all who call on Him,

verses.esv: For “everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved.”
verses.kjv: For whosoever shall call upon the name of the Lord shall be saved.
verses.bsb: for, “Everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved.”

verses.esv: How then will they call on him in whom they have not believed? And how are they to believe in him of whom they have never heard? And how are they to hear without someone preaching?
verses.kjv: How then shall they call on him in whom they have not believed? and how shall they believe in him of whom they have not heard? and how shall they hear without a preacher?
verses.bsb: How then can they call on the One in whom they have not believed? And how can they believe in the One of whom they have not heard? And how can they hear without someone to preach?

verses.esv: And how are they to preach unless they are sent? As it is written, “How beautiful are the feet of those who preach the good news!”
verses.kjv: And how shall they preach, except they be sent? as it is written, How beautiful are the feet of them that preach the gospel of peace, and bring glad tidings of good things!
verses.bsb: And how can they preach unless they are sent? As it is written: “How beautiful are the feet of those who bring good news!”

verses.esv: But they have not all obeyed the gospel. For Isaiah says, “Lord, who has believed what he has heard from us?”
verses.kjv: But they have not all obeyed the gospel. For Esaias saith, Lord, who hath believed our report?
verses.bsb: But not all of them welcomed the good news. For Isaiah says, “Lord, who has believed our message?”

verses.esv: So faith comes from hearing, and hearing through the word of Christ.
verses.kjv: So then faith cometh by hearing, and hearing by the word of God.
verses.bsb: Consequently, faith comes by hearing, and hearing by the word of Christ.

verses.esv: But I ask, have they not heard? Indeed they have, for “Their voice has gone out to all the earth, and their words to the ends of the world.”
verses.kjv: But I say, Have they not heard? Yes verily, their sound went into all the earth, and their words unto the ends of the world.
verses.bsb: But I ask, did they not hear? Indeed they did:  “Their voice has gone out into all the earth, their words to the ends of the world.”

verses.esv: But I ask, did Israel not understand? First Moses says, “I will make you jealous of those who are not a nation; with a foolish nation I will make you angry.”
verses.kjv: But I say, Did not Israel know? First Moses saith, I will provoke you to jealousy by them that are no people, and by a foolish nation I will anger you.
verses.bsb: I ask instead, did Israel not understand? First, Moses says:  “I will make you jealous by those who are not a nation; I will make you angry by a nation without understanding.”

verses.esv: Then Isaiah is so bold as to say, “I have been found by those who did not seek me; I have shown myself to those who did not ask for me.”
verses.kjv: But Esaias is very bold, and saith, I was found of them that sought me not; I was made manifest unto them that asked not after me.
verses.bsb: And Isaiah boldly says:  “I was found by those who did not seek Me; I revealed Myself to those who did not ask for Me.”

verses.esv: But of Israel he says, “All day long I have held out my hands to a disobedient and contrary people.”
verses.kjv: But to Israel he saith, All day long I have stretched forth my hands unto a disobedient and gainsaying people.
verses.bsb: But as for Israel he says:  “All day long I have held out My hands to a disobedient and obstinate people.”

verses.esv: I ask, then, has God rejected his people? By no means! For I myself am an Israelite, a descendant of Abraham, a member of the tribe of Benjamin.
verses.kjv: I SAY then, Hath God cast away his people? God forbid. For I also am an Israelite, of the seed of Abraham, of the tribe of Benjamin.
verses.bsb: I ask then, did God reject His people? Certainly not! I am an Israelite myself, a descendant of Abraham, from the tribe of Benjamin.

verses.esv: God has not rejected his people whom he foreknew. Do you not know what the Scripture says of Elijah, how he appeals to God against Israel?
verses.kjv: God hath not cast away his people which he foreknew. Wot ye not what the scripture saith of Elias? how he maketh intercession to God against Israel, saying,
verses.bsb: God did not reject His people, whom He foreknew. Do you not know what the Scripture says about Elijah, how he appealed to God against Israel:

verses.esv: “Lord, they have killed your prophets, they have demolished your altars, and I alone am left, and they seek my life.”
verses.kjv: Lord, they have killed thy prophets, and digged down thine altars; and I am left alone, and they seek my life.
verses.bsb: “Lord, they have killed Your prophets and torn down Your altars. I am the only one left, and they are seeking my life as well”?

verses.esv: But what is God's reply to him? “I have kept for myself seven thousand men who have not bowed the knee to Baal.”
verses.kjv: But what saith the answer of God unto him? I have reserved to myself seven thousand men, who have not bowed the knee to the image of Baal.
verses.bsb: And what was the divine reply to him? “I have reserved for Myself seven thousand men who have not bowed the knee to Baal.”

verses.esv: So too at the present time there is a remnant, chosen by grace.
verses.kjv: Even so then at this present time also there is a remnant according to the election of grace.
verses.bsb: In the same way, at the present time there is a remnant chosen by grace.

verses.esv: But if it is by grace, it is no longer on the basis of works; otherwise grace would no longer be grace.
verses.kjv: And if by grace, then is it no more of works: otherwise grace is no more grace. But if it be of works, then is it no more grace: otherwise work is no more work.
verses.bsb: And if it is by grace, then it is no longer by works. Otherwise, grace would no longer be grace.

verses.esv: What then? Israel failed to obtain what it was seeking. The elect obtained it, but the rest were hardened,
verses.kjv: What then? Israel hath not obtained that which he seeketh for; but the election hath obtained it, and the rest were blinded
verses.bsb: What then? What Israel was seeking, it failed to obtain, but the elect did. The others were hardened,

verses.esv: as it is written, “God gave them a spirit of stupor, eyes that would not see and ears that would not hear, down to this very day.”
verses.kjv: (According as it is written, God hath given them the spirit of slumber, eyes that they should not see, and ears that they should not hear;) unto this day.
verses.bsb: as it is written:  “God gave them a spirit of stupor, eyes that could not see, and ears that could not hear, to this very day.”

verses.esv: And David says, “Let their table become a snare and a trap, a stumbling block and a retribution for them;
verses.kjv: And David saith, Let their table be made a snare, and a trap, and a stumblingblock, and a recompence unto them:
verses.bsb: And David says:  “May their table become a snare and a trap, a stumbling block and a retribution to them.

verses.esv: let their eyes be darkened so that they cannot see, and bend their backs forever.”
verses.kjv: Let their eyes be darkened, that they may not see, and bow down their back alway.
verses.bsb: May their eyes be darkened so they cannot see, and their backs be bent forever.”

verses.esv: So I ask, did they stumble in order that they might fall? By no means! Rather, through their trespass salvation has come to the Gentiles, so as to make Israel jealous.
verses.kjv: I say then, Have they stumbled that they should fall? God forbid: but rather through their fall salvation is come unto the Gentiles, for to provoke them to jealousy.
verses.bsb: I ask then, did they stumble so as to fall beyond recovery? Certainly not! However, because of their trespass, salvation has come to the Gentiles to make Israel jealous.

verses.esv: Now if their trespass means riches for the world, and if their failure means riches for the Gentiles, how much more will their full inclusion mean!
verses.kjv: Now if the fall of them be the riches of the world, and the diminishing of them the riches of the Gentiles; how much more their fulness?
verses.bsb: But if their trespass means riches for the world, and their failure means riches for the Gentiles, how much greater riches will their fullness bring!

verses.esv: Now I am speaking to you Gentiles. Inasmuch then as I am an apostle to the Gentiles, I magnify my ministry
verses.kjv: For I speak to you Gentiles, inasmuch as I am the apostle of the Gentiles, I magnify mine office:
verses.bsb: I am speaking to you Gentiles. Inasmuch as I am the apostle to the Gentiles, I magnify my ministry

verses.esv: in order somehow to make my fellow Jews jealous, and thus save some of them.
verses.kjv: If by any means I may provoke to emulation them which are my flesh, and might save some of them.
verses.bsb: in the hope that I may provoke my own people to jealousy and save some of them.

verses.esv: For if their rejection means the reconciliation of the world, what will their acceptance mean but life from the dead?
verses.kjv: For if the casting away of them be the reconciling of the world, what shall the receiving of them be, but life from the dead?
verses.bsb: For if their rejection is the reconciliation of the world, what will their acceptance be but life from the dead?

verses.esv: If the dough offered as firstfruits is holy, so is the whole lump, and if the root is holy, so are the branches.
verses.kjv: For if the firstfruit be holy, the lump is also holy: and if the root be holy, so are the branches.
verses.bsb: If the first part of the dough is holy, so is the whole batch; if the root is holy, so are the branches.

verses.esv: But if some of the branches were broken off, and you, although a wild olive shoot, were grafted in among the others and now share in the nourishing root of the olive tree,
verses.kjv: And if some of the branches be broken off, and thou, being a wild olive tree, wert graffed in among them, and with them partakest of the root and fatness of the olive tree;
verses.bsb: Now if some branches have been broken off, and you, a wild olive shoot, have been grafted in among the others to share in the nourishment of the olive root,

verses.esv: do not be arrogant toward the branches. If you are, remember it is not you who support the root, but the root that supports you.
verses.kjv: Boast not against the branches. But if thou boast, thou bearest not the root, but the root thee.
verses.bsb: do not boast over those branches. If you do, remember this: You do not support the root, but the root supports you.

verses.esv: Then you will say, “Branches were broken off so that I might be grafted in.”
verses.kjv: Thou wilt say then, The branches were broken off, that I might be graffed in.
verses.bsb: You will say then, “Branches were broken off so that I could be grafted in.”

verses.esv: That is true. They were broken off because of their unbelief, but you stand fast through faith. So do not become proud, but fear.
verses.kjv: Well; because of unbelief they were broken off, and thou standest by faith. Be not highminded, but fear:
verses.bsb: That is correct: They were broken off because of unbelief, but you stand by faith. Do not be arrogant, but be afraid.

verses.esv: For if God did not spare the natural branches, neither will he spare you.
verses.kjv: For if God spared not the natural branches, take heed lest he also spare not thee.
verses.bsb: For if God did not spare the natural branches, He will certainly not spare you either.

verses.esv: Note then the kindness and the severity of God: severity toward those who have fallen, but God's kindness to you, provided you continue in his kindness. Otherwise you too will be cut off.
verses.kjv: Behold therefore the goodness and severity of God: on them which fell, severity; but toward thee, goodness, if thou continue in his goodness: otherwise thou also shalt be cut off.
verses.bsb: Take notice, therefore, of the kindness and severity of God: severity to those who fell, but kindness to you, if you continue in His kindness. Otherwise you also will be cut off.

verses.esv: And even they, if they do not continue in their unbelief, will be grafted in, for God has the power to graft them in again.
verses.kjv: And they also, if they abide not still in unbelief, shall be graffed in: for God is able to graff them in again.
verses.bsb: And if they do not persist in unbelief, they will be grafted in, for God is able to graft them in again.

verses.esv: For if you were cut from what is by nature a wild olive tree, and grafted, contrary to nature, into a cultivated olive tree, how much more will these, the natural branches, be grafted back into their own olive tree.
verses.kjv: For if thou wert cut out of the olive tree which is wild by nature, and wert graffed contrary to nature into a good olive tree: how much more shall these, which be the natural branches, be graffed into their own olive tree?
verses.bsb: For if you were cut from a wild olive tree, and contrary to nature were grafted into one that is cultivated, how much more readily will these, the natural branches, be grafted into their own olive tree!

verses.esv: Lest you be wise in your own sight, I do not want you to be unaware of this mystery, brothers: a partial hardening has come upon Israel, until the fullness of the Gentiles has come in.
verses.kjv: For I would not, brethren, that ye should be ignorant of this mystery, lest ye should be wise in your own conceits; that blindness in part is happened to Israel, until the fulness of the Gentiles be come in.
verses.bsb: I do not want you to be ignorant of this mystery, brothers, so that you will not be conceited: A hardening in part has come to Israel, until the full number of the Gentiles has come in.

verses.esv: And in this way all Israel will be saved, as it is written, “The Deliverer will come from Zion, he will banish ungodliness from Jacob”;
verses.kjv: And so all Israel shall be saved: as it is written, There shall come out of Sion the Deliverer, and shall turn away ungodliness from Jacob:
verses.bsb: And so all Israel will be saved, as it is written:  “The Deliverer will come from Zion; He will remove godlessness from Jacob.

verses.esv: “and this will be my covenant with them when I take away their sins.”
verses.kjv: For this is my covenant unto them, when I shall take away their sins.
verses.bsb: And this is My covenant with them when I take away their sins.”

verses.esv: As regards the gospel, they are enemies for your sake. But as regards election, they are beloved for the sake of their forefathers.
verses.kjv: As concerning the gospel, they are enemies for your sakes: but as touching the election, they are beloved for the fathers’ sakes.
verses.bsb: Regarding the gospel, they are enemies on your account; but regarding election, they are loved on account of the patriarchs.

verses.esv: For the gifts and the calling of God are irrevocable.
verses.kjv: For the gifts and calling of God are without repentance.
verses.bsb: For God’s gifts and His call are irrevocable.

verses.esv: For just as you were at one time disobedient to God but now have received mercy because of their disobedience,
verses.kjv: For as ye in times past have not believed God, yet have now obtained mercy through their unbelief:
verses.bsb: Just as you who formerly disobeyed God have now received mercy through their disobedience,

verses.esv: so they too have now been disobedient in order that by the mercy shown to you they also may now receive mercy.
verses.kjv: Even so have these also now not believed, that through your mercy they also may obtain mercy.
verses.bsb: so they too have now disobeyed, in order that they too may now receive mercy through the mercy shown to you.

verses.esv: For God has consigned all to disobedience, that he may have mercy on all.
verses.kjv: For God hath concluded them all in unbelief, that he might have mercy upon all.
verses.bsb: For God has consigned everyone to disobedience so that He may have mercy on everyone.

verses.esv: Oh, the depth of the riches and wisdom and knowledge of God! How unsearchable are his judgments and how inscrutable his ways!
verses.kjv: O the depth of the riches both of the wisdom and knowledge of God! how unsearchable are his judgments, and his ways past finding out!
verses.bsb: O, the depth of the riches of the wisdom and knowledge of God! How unsearchable are His judgments, and untraceable His ways!

verses.esv: “For who has known the mind of the Lord, or who has been his counselor?”
verses.kjv: For who hath known the mind of the Lord? or who hath been his counseller?
verses.bsb: “Who has known the mind of the Lord? Or who has been His counselor?”

verses.esv: “Or who has given a gift to him that he might be repaid?”
verses.kjv: Or who hath first given to him, and it shall be recompensed unto him again?
verses.bsb: “Who has first given to God, that God should repay him?”

verses.esv: For from him and through him and to him are all things. To him be glory forever. Amen.
verses.kjv: For of him, and through him, and to him, are all things: to whom be glory for ever. Amen.
verses.bsb: For from Him and through Him and to Him are all things. To Him be the glory forever! Amen.

verses.esv: I appeal to you therefore, brothers, by the mercies of God, to present your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and acceptable to God, which is your spiritual worship.
verses.kjv: I BESEECH you therefore, brethren, by the mercies of God, that ye present your bodies a living sacrifice, holy, acceptable unto God, which is your reasonable service.
verses.bsb: Therefore I urge you, brothers, on account of God’s mercy, to offer your bodies as living sacrifices, holy and pleasing to God, which is your spiritual service of worship.

verses.esv: Do not be conformed to this world, but be transformed by the renewal of your mind, that by testing you may discern what is the will of God, what is good and acceptable and perfect.
verses.kjv: And be not conformed to this world: but be ye transformed by the renewing of your mind, that ye may prove what is that good, and acceptable, and perfect, will of God.
verses.bsb: Do not be conformed to this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind. Then you will be able to test and approve what is the good, pleasing, and perfect will of God.

verses.esv: For by the grace given to me I say to everyone among you not to think of himself more highly than he ought to think, but to think with sober judgment, each according to the measure of faith that God has assigned.
verses.kjv: For I say, through the grace given unto me, to every man that is among you, not to think of himself more highly than he ought to think; but to think soberly, according as God hath dealt to every man the measure of faith.
verses.bsb: For by the grace given me I say to every one of you: Do not think of yourself more highly than you ought, but think of yourself with sober judgment, according to the measure of faith God has given you.

verses.esv: For as in one body we have many members, and the members do not all have the same function,
verses.kjv: For as we have many members in one body, and all members have not the same office:
verses.bsb: Just as each of us has one body with many members, and not all members have the same function,

verses.esv: so we, though many, are one body in Christ, and individually members one of another.
verses.kjv: So we, being many, are one body in Christ, and every one members one of another.
verses.bsb: so in Christ we who are many are one body, and each member belongs to one another.

verses.esv: Having gifts that differ according to the grace given to us, let us use them: if prophecy, in proportion to our faith;
verses.kjv: Having then gifts differing according to the grace that is given to us, whether prophecy, let us prophesy according to the proportion of faith;
verses.bsb: We have different gifts according to the grace given us. If one’s gift is prophecy, let him use it in proportion to his faith;

verses.esv: if service, in our serving; the one who teaches, in his teaching;
verses.kjv: Or ministry, let us wait on our ministering: or he that teacheth, on teaching;
verses.bsb: if it is serving, let him serve; if it is teaching, let him teach;

verses.esv: the one who exhorts, in his exhortation; the one who contributes, in generosity; the one who leads, with zeal; the one who does acts of mercy, with cheerfulness.
verses.kjv: Or he that exhorteth, on exhortation: he that giveth, let him do it with simplicity; he that ruleth, with diligence; he that sheweth mercy, with cheerfulness.
verses.bsb: if it is encouraging, let him encourage; if it is giving, let him give generously; if it is leading, let him lead with diligence; if it is showing mercy, let him do it cheerfully.

verses.esv: Let love be genuine. Abhor what is evil; hold fast to what is good.
verses.kjv: Let love be without dissimulation. Abhor that which is evil; cleave to that which is good.
verses.bsb: Love must be sincere. Detest what is evil; cling to what is good.

verses.esv: Love one another with brotherly affection. Outdo one another in showing honor.
verses.kjv: Be kindly affectioned one to another with brotherly love; in honour preferring one another;
verses.bsb: Be devoted to one another in brotherly love. Outdo yourselves in honoring one another.

verses.esv: Do not be slothful in zeal, be fervent in spirit, serve the Lord.
verses.kjv: Not slothful in business; fervent in spirit; serving the Lord;
verses.bsb: Do not let your zeal subside; keep your spiritual fervor, serving the Lord.

verses.esv: Rejoice in hope, be patient in tribulation, be constant in prayer.
verses.kjv: Rejoicing in hope; patient in tribulation; continuing instant in prayer;
verses.bsb: Be joyful in hope, patient in affliction, persistent in prayer.

verses.esv: Contribute to the needs of the saints and seek to show hospitality.
verses.kjv: Distributing to the necessity of saints; given to hospitality.
verses.bsb: Share with the saints who are in need. Practice hospitality.

verses.esv: Bless those who persecute you; bless and do not curse them.
verses.kjv: Bless them which persecute you: bless, and curse not.
verses.bsb: Bless those who persecute you. Bless and do not curse.

verses.esv: Rejoice with those who rejoice, weep with those who weep.
verses.kjv: Rejoice with them that do rejoice, and weep with them that weep.
verses.bsb: Rejoice with those who rejoice; weep with those who weep.

verses.esv: Live in harmony with one another. Do not be haughty, but associate with the lowly. Never be wise in your own sight.
verses.kjv: Be of the same mind one toward another. Mind not high things, but condescend to men of low estate. Be not wise in your own conceits.
verses.bsb: Live in harmony with one another. Do not be proud, but enjoy the company of the lowly. Do not be conceited.

verses.esv: Repay no one evil for evil, but give thought to do what is honorable in the sight of all.
verses.kjv: Recompense to no man evil for evil. Provide things honest in the sight of all men.
verses.bsb: Do not repay anyone evil for evil. Carefully consider what is right in the eyes of everybody.

verses.esv: If possible, so far as it depends on you, live peaceably with all.
verses.kjv: If it be possible, as much as lieth in you, live peaceably with all men.
verses.bsb: If it is possible on your part, live at peace with everyone.

verses.esv: Beloved, never avenge yourselves, but leave it to the wrath of God, for it is written, “Vengeance is mine, I will repay, says the Lord.”
verses.kjv: Dearly beloved, avenge not yourselves, but rather give place unto wrath: for it is written, Vengeance is mine; I will repay, saith the Lord.
verses.bsb: Do not avenge yourselves, beloved, but leave room for God’s wrath. For it is written: “Vengeance is Mine; I will repay, says the Lord.”

verses.esv: To the contrary, “if your enemy is hungry, feed him; if he is thirsty, give him something to drink; for by so doing you will heap burning coals on his head.”
verses.kjv: Therefore if thine enemy hunger, feed him; if he thirst, give him drink: for in so doing thou shalt heap coals of fire on his head.
verses.bsb: On the contrary,  “If your enemy is hungry, feed him; if he is thirsty, give him a drink. For in so doing, you will heap burning coals on his head.”

verses.esv: Do not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good.
verses.kjv: Be not overcome of evil, but overcome evil with good.
verses.bsb: Do not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good.

verses.esv: Let every person be subject to the governing authorities. For there is no authority except from God, and those that exist have been instituted by God.
verses.kjv: LET every soul be subject unto the higher powers. For there is no power but of God: the powers that be are ordained of God.
verses.bsb: Everyone must submit himself to the governing authorities, for there is no authority except that which is from God. The authorities that exist have been appointed by God.

verses.esv: Therefore whoever resists the authorities resists what God has appointed, and those who resist will incur judgment.
verses.kjv: Whosoever therefore resisteth the power, resisteth the ordinance of God: and they that resist shall receive to themselves damnation.
verses.bsb: Consequently, whoever resists authority is opposing what God has set in place, and those who do so will bring judgment on themselves.

verses.esv: For rulers are not a terror to good conduct, but to bad. Would you have no fear of the one who is in authority? Then do what is good, and you will receive his approval,
verses.kjv: For rulers are not a terror to good works, but to the evil. Wilt thou then not be afraid of the power? do that which is good, and thou shalt have praise of the same:
verses.bsb: For rulers are not a terror to good conduct, but to bad. Do you want to be unafraid of the one in authority? Then do what is right, and you will have his approval.

verses.esv: for he is God's servant for your good. But if you do wrong, be afraid, for he does not bear the sword in vain. For he is the servant of God, an avenger who carries out God's wrath on the wrongdoer.
verses.kjv: For he is the minister of God to thee for good. But if thou do that which is evil, be afraid; for he beareth not the sword in vain: for he is the minister of God, a revenger to execute wrath upon him that doeth evil.
verses.bsb: For he is God’s servant for your good. But if you do wrong, be afraid, for he does not carry the sword in vain. He is God’s servant, an agent of retribution to the wrongdoer.

verses.esv: Therefore one must be in subjection, not only to avoid God's wrath but also for the sake of conscience.
verses.kjv: Wherefore ye must needs be subject, not only for wrath, but also for conscience sake.
verses.bsb: Therefore it is necessary to submit to authority, not only to avoid punishment, but also as a matter of conscience.

verses.esv: For because of this you also pay taxes, for the authorities are ministers of God, attending to this very thing.
verses.kjv: For for this cause pay ye tribute also: for they are God’s ministers, attending continually upon this very thing.
verses.bsb: This is also why you pay taxes. For the authorities are God’s servants, who devote themselves to their work.

verses.esv: Pay to all what is owed to them: taxes to whom taxes are owed, revenue to whom revenue is owed, respect to whom respect is owed, honor to whom honor is owed.
verses.kjv: Render therefore to all their dues: tribute to whom tribute is due; custom to whom custom; fear to whom fear; honour to whom honour.
verses.bsb: Pay everyone what you owe him: taxes to whom taxes are due, revenue to whom revenue is due, respect to whom respect is due, honor to whom honor is due.

verses.esv: Owe no one anything, except to love each other, for the one who loves another has fulfilled the law.
verses.kjv: Owe no man any thing, but to love one another: for he that loveth another hath fulfilled the law.
verses.bsb: Be indebted to no one, except to one another in love. For he who loves his neighbor has fulfilled the law.

verses.esv: For the commandments, “You shall not commit adultery, You shall not murder, You shall not steal, You shall not covet,” and any other commandment, are summed up in this word: “You shall love your neighbor as yourself.”
verses.kjv: For this, Thou shalt not commit adultery, Thou shalt not kill, Thou shalt not steal, Thou shalt not bear false witness, Thou shalt not covet; and if there be any other commandment, it is briefly comprehended in this saying, namely, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself.
verses.bsb: The commandments “Do not commit adultery,” “Do not murder,” “Do not steal,” “Do not covet,” and any other commandments, are summed up in this one decree: “Love your neighbor as yourself.”

verses.esv: Love does no wrong to a neighbor; therefore love is the fulfilling of the law.
verses.kjv: Love worketh no ill to his neighbour: therefore love is the fulfilling of the law.
verses.bsb: Love does no wrong to its neighbor. Therefore love is the fulfillment of the law.

verses.esv: Besides this you know the time, that the hour has come for you to wake from sleep. For salvation is nearer to us now than when we first believed.
verses.kjv: And that, knowing the time, that now it is high time to awake out of sleep: for now is our salvation nearer than when we believed.
verses.bsb: And do this, understanding the occasion. The hour has come for you to wake up from your slumber, for our salvation is nearer now than when we first believed.

verses.esv: The night is far gone; the day is at hand. So then let us cast off the works of darkness and put on the armor of light.
verses.kjv: The night is far spent, the day is at hand: let us therefore cast off the works of darkness, and let us put on the armour of light.
verses.bsb: The night is nearly over; the day has drawn near. So let us lay aside the deeds of darkness and put on the armor of light.

verses.esv: Let us walk properly as in the daytime, not in orgies and drunkenness, not in sexual immorality and sensuality, not in quarreling and jealousy.
verses.kjv: Let us walk honestly, as in the day; not in rioting and drunkenness, not in chambering and wantonness, not in strife and envying.
verses.bsb: Let us behave decently, as in the daytime, not in carousing and drunkenness, not in sexual immorality and debauchery, not in dissension and jealousy.

verses.esv: But put on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make no provision for the flesh, to gratify its desires.
verses.kjv: But put ye on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make not provision for the flesh, to fulfil the lusts thereof.
verses.bsb: Instead, clothe yourselves with the Lord Jesus Christ, and make no provision for the desires of the flesh.

verses.esv: As for the one who is weak in faith, welcome him, but not to quarrel over opinions.
verses.kjv: HIM that is weak in the faith receive ye, but not to doubtful disputations.
verses.bsb: Accept him whose faith is weak, without passing judgment on his opinions.

verses.esv: One person believes he may eat anything, while the weak person eats only vegetables.
verses.kjv: For one believeth that he may eat all things: another, who is weak, eateth herbs.
verses.bsb: For one person has faith to eat all things, while another, who is weak, eats only vegetables.

verses.esv: Let not the one who eats despise the one who abstains, and let not the one who abstains pass judgment on the one who eats, for God has welcomed him.
verses.kjv: Let not him that eateth despise him that eateth not; and let not him which eateth not judge him that eateth: for God hath received him.
verses.bsb: The one who eats everything must not belittle the one who does not, and the one who does not eat everything must not judge the one who does, for God has accepted him.

verses.esv: Who are you to pass judgment on the servant of another? It is before his own master that he stands or falls. And he will be upheld, for the Lord is able to make him stand.
verses.kjv: Who art thou that judgest another man’s servant? to his own master he standeth or falleth. Yea, he shall be holden up: for God is able to make him stand.
verses.bsb: Who are you to judge someone else’s servant? To his own master he stands or falls. And he will stand, for the Lord is able to make him stand.

verses.esv: One person esteems one day as better than another, while another esteems all days alike. Each one should be fully convinced in his own mind.
verses.kjv: One man esteemeth one day above another: another esteemeth every day alike. Let every man be fully persuaded in his own mind.
verses.bsb: One person regards a certain day above the others, while someone else considers every day alike. Each one should be fully convinced in his own mind.

verses.esv: The one who observes the day, observes it in honor of the Lord. The one who eats, eats in honor of the Lord, since he gives thanks to God, while the one who abstains, abstains in honor of the Lord and gives thanks to God.
verses.kjv: He that regardeth the day, regardeth it unto the Lord; and he that regardeth not the day, to the Lord he doth not regard it. He that eateth, eateth to the Lord, for he giveth God thanks; and he that eateth not, to the Lord he eateth not, and giveth God thanks.
verses.bsb: He who observes a special day does so to the Lord; he who eats does so to the Lord, for he gives thanks to God; and he who abstains does so to the Lord and gives thanks to God.

verses.esv: For none of us lives to himself, and none of us dies to himself.
verses.kjv: For none of us liveth to himself, and no man dieth to himself.
verses.bsb: For none of us lives to himself alone, and none of us dies to himself alone.

verses.esv: For if we live, we live to the Lord, and if we die, we die to the Lord. So then, whether we live or whether we die, we are the Lord's.
verses.kjv: For whether we live, we live unto the Lord; and whether we die, we die unto the Lord: whether we live therefore, or die, we are the Lord’s.
verses.bsb: If we live, we live to the Lord, and if we die, we die to the Lord. So whether we live or die, we belong to the Lord.

verses.esv: For to this end Christ died and lived again, that he might be Lord both of the dead and of the living.
verses.kjv: For to this end Christ both died, and rose, and revived, that he might be Lord both of the dead and living.
verses.bsb: For this reason Christ died and returned to life, that He might be the Lord of both the dead and the living.

verses.esv: Why do you pass judgment on your brother? Or you, why do you despise your brother? For we will all stand before the judgment seat of God;
verses.kjv: But why dost thou judge thy brother? or why dost thou set at nought thy brother? for we shall all stand before the judgment seat of Christ.
verses.bsb: Why, then, do you judge your brother? Or why do you belittle your brother? For we will all stand before God’s judgment seat.

verses.esv: for it is written, “As I live, says the Lord, every knee shall bow to me, and every tongue shall confess to God.”
verses.kjv: For it is written, As I live, saith the Lord, every knee shall bow to me, and every tongue shall confess to God.
verses.bsb: It is written:  “As surely as I live, says the Lord, every knee will bow before Me; every tongue will confess to God.”

verses.esv: So then each of us will give an account of himself to God.
verses.kjv: So then every one of us shall give account of himself to God.
verses.bsb: So then, each of us will give an account of himself to God.

verses.esv: Therefore let us not pass judgment on one another any longer, but rather decide never to put a stumbling block or hindrance in the way of a brother.
verses.kjv: Let us not therefore judge one another any more: but judge this rather, that no man put a stumblingblock or an occasion to fall in his brother’s way.
verses.bsb: Therefore let us stop judging one another. Instead, make up your mind not to put any stumbling block or obstacle in your brother’s way.

verses.esv: I know and am persuaded in the Lord Jesus that nothing is unclean in itself, but it is unclean for anyone who thinks it unclean.
verses.kjv: I know, and am persuaded by the Lord Jesus, that there is nothing unclean of itself: but to him that esteemeth any thing to be unclean, to him it is unclean.
verses.bsb: I am convinced and fully persuaded in the Lord Jesus that nothing is unclean in itself. But if anyone regards something as unclean, then for him it is unclean.

verses.esv: For if your brother is grieved by what you eat, you are no longer walking in love. By what you eat, do not destroy the one for whom Christ died.
verses.kjv: But if thy brother be grieved with thy meat, now walkest thou not charitably. Destroy not him with thy meat, for whom Christ died.
verses.bsb: If your brother is distressed by what you eat, you are no longer acting in love. Do not by your eating destroy your brother, for whom Christ died.

verses.esv: So do not let what you regard as good be spoken of as evil.
verses.kjv: Let not then your good be evil spoken of:
verses.bsb: Do not allow what you consider good, then, to be spoken of as evil.

verses.esv: For the kingdom of God is not a matter of eating and drinking but of righteousness and peace and joy in the Holy Spirit.
verses.kjv: For the kingdom of God is not meat and drink; but righteousness, and peace, and joy in the Holy Ghost.
verses.bsb: For the kingdom of God is not a matter of eating and drinking, but of righteousness, peace, and joy in the Holy Spirit.

verses.esv: Whoever thus serves Christ is acceptable to God and approved by men.
verses.kjv: For he that in these things serveth Christ is acceptable to God, and approved of men.
verses.bsb: For whoever serves Christ in this way is pleasing to God and approved by men.

verses.esv: So then let us pursue what makes for peace and for mutual upbuilding.
verses.kjv: Let us therefore follow after the things which make for peace, and things wherewith one may edify another.
verses.bsb: So then, let us pursue what leads to peace and to mutual edification.

verses.esv: Do not, for the sake of food, destroy the work of God. Everything is indeed clean, but it is wrong for anyone to make another stumble by what he eats.
verses.kjv: For meat destroy not the work of God. All things indeed are pure; but it is evil for that man who eateth with offence.
verses.bsb: Do not destroy the work of God for the sake of food. All food is clean, but it is wrong for a man to let his eating be a stumbling block.

verses.esv: It is good not to eat meat or drink wine or do anything that causes your brother to stumble.
verses.kjv: It is good neither to eat flesh, nor to drink wine, nor any thing whereby thy brother stumbleth, or is offended, or is made weak.
verses.bsb: It is better not to eat meat or drink wine or to do anything to cause your brother to stumble.

verses.esv: The faith that you have, keep between yourself and God. Blessed is the one who has no reason to pass judgment on himself for what he approves.
verses.kjv: Hast thou faith? have it to thyself before God. Happy is he that condemneth not himself in that thing which he alloweth.
verses.bsb: Keep your belief about such matters between yourself and God. Blessed is the one who does not condemn himself by what he approves.

verses.esv: But whoever has doubts is condemned if he eats, because the eating is not from faith. For whatever does not proceed from faith is sin.
verses.kjv: And he that doubteth is damned if he eat, because he eateth not of faith: for whatsoever is not of faith is sin.
verses.bsb: But the one who has doubts is condemned if he eats, because his eating is not from faith; and everything that is not from faith is sin.

verses.esv: We who are strong have an obligation to bear with the failings of the weak, and not to please ourselves.
verses.kjv: WE then that are strong ought to bear the infirmities of the weak, and not to please ourselves.
verses.bsb: We who are strong ought to bear with the shortcomings of the weak and not to please ourselves.

verses.esv: Let each of us please his neighbor for his good, to build him up.
verses.kjv: Let every one of us please his neighbour for his good to edification.
verses.bsb: Each of us should please his neighbor for his good, to build him up.

verses.esv: For Christ did not please himself, but as it is written, “The reproaches of those who reproached you fell on me.”
verses.kjv: For even Christ pleased not himself; but, as it is written, The reproaches of them that reproached thee fell on me.
verses.bsb: For even Christ did not please Himself, but as it is written: “The insults of those who insult You have fallen on Me.”

verses.esv: For whatever was written in former days was written for our instruction, that through endurance and through the encouragement of the Scriptures we might have hope.
verses.kjv: For whatsoever things were written aforetime were written for our learning, that we through patience and comfort of the scriptures might have hope.
verses.bsb: For everything that was written in the past was written for our instruction, so that through endurance and the encouragement of the Scriptures, we might have hope.

verses.esv: May the God of endurance and encouragement grant you to live in such harmony with one another, in accord with Christ Jesus,
verses.kjv: Now the God of patience and consolation grant you to be likeminded one toward another according to Christ Jesus:
verses.bsb: Now may the God who gives endurance and encouragement grant you harmony with one another in Christ Jesus,

verses.esv: that together you may with one voice glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
verses.kjv: That ye may with one mind and one mouth glorify God, even the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
verses.bsb: so that with one mind and one voice you may glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.esv: Therefore welcome one another as Christ has welcomed you, for the glory of God.
verses.kjv: Wherefore receive ye one another, as Christ also received us to the glory of God.
verses.bsb: Accept one another, then, just as Christ accepted you, in order to bring glory to God.

verses.esv: For I tell you that Christ became a servant to the circumcised to show God's truthfulness, in order to confirm the promises given to the patriarchs,
verses.kjv: Now I say that Jesus Christ was a minister of the circumcision for the truth of God, to confirm the promises made unto the fathers:
verses.bsb: For I tell you that Christ has become a servant of the circumcised on behalf of God’s truth, to confirm the promises made to the patriarchs,

verses.esv: and in order that the Gentiles might glorify God for his mercy. As it is written, “Therefore I will praise you among the Gentiles, and sing to your name.”
verses.kjv: And that the Gentiles might glorify God for his mercy; as it is written, For this cause I will confess to thee among the Gentiles, and sing unto thy name.
verses.bsb: so that the Gentiles may glorify God for His mercy. As it is written:  “Therefore I will praise You among the Gentiles; I will sing hymns to Your name.”

verses.esv: And again it is said, “Rejoice, O Gentiles, with his people.”
verses.kjv: And again he saith, Rejoice, ye Gentiles, with his people.
verses.bsb: Again, it says:  “Rejoice, O Gentiles, with His people.”

verses.esv: And again, “Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles, and let all the peoples extol him.”
verses.kjv: And again, Praise the Lord, all ye Gentiles; and laud him, all ye people.
verses.bsb: And again:  “Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles, and extol Him, all you peoples.”

verses.esv: And again Isaiah says, “The root of Jesse will come, even he who arises to rule the Gentiles; in him will the Gentiles hope.”
verses.kjv: And again, Esaias saith, There shall be a root of Jesse, and he that shall rise to reign over the Gentiles; in him shall the Gentiles trust.
verses.bsb: And once more, Isaiah says:  “The Root of Jesse will appear, One who will arise to rule over the Gentiles; in Him the Gentiles will put their hope.”

verses.esv: May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, so that by the power of the Holy Spirit you may abound in hope.
verses.kjv: Now the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, that ye may abound in hope, through the power of the Holy Ghost.
verses.bsb: Now may the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace as you believe in Him, so that you may overflow with hope by the power of the Holy Spirit.

verses.esv: I myself am satisfied about you, my brothers, that you yourselves are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge and able to instruct one another.
verses.kjv: And I myself also am persuaded of you, my brethren, that ye also are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, able also to admonish one another.
verses.bsb: I myself am convinced, my brothers, that you yourselves are full of goodness, brimming with knowledge, and able to instruct one another.

verses.esv: But on some points I have written to you very boldly by way of reminder, because of the grace given me by God
verses.kjv: Nevertheless, brethren, I have written the more boldly unto you in some sort, as putting you in mind, because of the grace that is given to me of God,
verses.bsb: However, I have written you a bold reminder on some points, because of the grace God has given me

verses.esv: to be a minister of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles in the priestly service of the gospel of God, so that the offering of the Gentiles may be acceptable, sanctified by the Holy Spirit.
verses.kjv: That I should be the minister of Jesus Christ to the Gentiles, ministering the gospel of God, that the offering up of the Gentiles might be acceptable, being sanctified by the Holy Ghost.
verses.bsb: to be a minister of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles in the priestly service of the gospel of God, so that the Gentiles might become an offering acceptable to God, sanctified by the Holy Spirit.

verses.esv: In Christ Jesus, then, I have reason to be proud of my work for God.
verses.kjv: I have therefore whereof I may glory through Jesus Christ in those things which pertain to God.
verses.bsb: Therefore I exult in Christ Jesus in my service to God.

verses.esv: For I will not venture to speak of anything except what Christ has accomplished through me to bring the Gentiles to obedience—by word and deed,
verses.kjv: For I will not dare to speak of any of those things which Christ hath not wrought by me, to make the Gentiles obedient, by word and deed,
verses.bsb: I will not presume to speak of anything except what Christ has accomplished through me in leading the Gentiles to obedience by word and deed,

verses.esv: by the power of signs and wonders, by the power of the Spirit of God—so that from Jerusalem and all the way around to Illyricum I have fulfilled the ministry of the gospel of Christ;
verses.kjv: Through mighty signs and wonders, by the power of the Spirit of God; so that from Jerusalem, and round about unto Illyricum, I have fully preached the gospel of Christ.
verses.bsb: by the power of signs and wonders, and by the power of the Spirit of God. So from Jerusalem all the way around to Illyricum, I have fully proclaimed the gospel of Christ.

verses.esv: and thus I make it my ambition to preach the gospel, not where Christ has already been named, lest I build on someone else's foundation,
verses.kjv: Yea, so have I strived to preach the gospel, not where Christ was named, lest I should build upon another man’s foundation:
verses.bsb: In this way I have aspired to preach the gospel where Christ was not known, so that I would not be building on someone else’s foundation.

verses.esv: but as it is written, “Those who have never been told of him will see, and those who have never heard will understand.”
verses.kjv: But as it is written, To whom he was not spoken of, they shall see: and they that have not heard shall understand.
verses.bsb: Rather, as it is written:  “Those who were not told about Him will see, and those who have not heard will understand.”

verses.esv: This is the reason why I have so often been hindered from coming to you.
verses.kjv: For which cause also I have been much hindered from coming to you.
verses.bsb: That is why I have often been hindered from coming to you.

verses.esv: But now, since I no longer have any room for work in these regions, and since I have longed for many years to come to you,
verses.kjv: But now having no more place in these parts, and having a great desire these many years to come unto you;
verses.bsb: But now that there are no further opportunities for me in these regions, and since I have longed for many years to visit you,

verses.esv: I hope to see you in passing as I go to Spain, and to be helped on my journey there by you, once I have enjoyed your company for a while.
verses.kjv: Whensoever I take my journey into Spain, I will come to you: for I trust to see you in my journey, and to be brought on my way thitherward by you, if first I be somewhat filled with your company.
verses.bsb: I hope to see you on my way to Spain. And after I have enjoyed your company for a while, you can equip me for my journey.

verses.esv: At present, however, I am going to Jerusalem bringing aid to the saints.
verses.kjv: But now I go unto Jerusalem to minister unto the saints.
verses.bsb: Now, however, I am on my way to Jerusalem to serve the saints there.

verses.esv: For Macedonia and Achaia have been pleased to make some contribution for the poor among the saints at Jerusalem.
verses.kjv: For it hath pleased them of Macedonia and Achaia to make a certain contribution for the poor saints which are at Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: For Macedonia and Achaia were pleased to make a contribution for the poor among the saints in Jerusalem.

verses.esv: For they were pleased to do it, and indeed they owe it to them. For if the Gentiles have come to share in their spiritual blessings, they ought also to be of service to them in material blessings.
verses.kjv: It hath pleased them verily; and their debtors they are. For if the Gentiles have been made partakers of their spiritual things, their duty is also to minister unto them in carnal things.
verses.bsb: They were pleased to do it, and indeed they owe it to them. For if the Gentiles have shared in their spiritual blessings, they are obligated to minister to them with material blessings.

verses.esv: When therefore I have completed this and have delivered to them what has been collected, I will leave for Spain by way of you.
verses.kjv: When therefore I have performed this, and have sealed to them this fruit, I will come by you into Spain.
verses.bsb: So after I have completed this service and have safely delivered this bounty to them, I will set off to Spain by way of you.

verses.esv: I know that when I come to you I will come in the fullness of the blessing of Christ.
verses.kjv: And I am sure that, when I come unto you, I shall come in the fulness of the blessing of the gospel of Christ.
verses.bsb: I know that when I come to you, I will come in the fullness of the blessing of Christ.

verses.esv: I appeal to you, brothers, by our Lord Jesus Christ and by the love of the Spirit, to strive together with me in your prayers to God on my behalf,
verses.kjv: Now I beseech you, brethren, for the Lord Jesus Christ’s sake, and for the love of the Spirit, that ye strive together with me in your prayers to God for me;
verses.bsb: Now I urge you, brothers, by our Lord Jesus Christ and by the love of the Spirit, to join me in my struggle by praying to God for me.

verses.esv: that I may be delivered from the unbelievers in Judea, and that my service for Jerusalem may be acceptable to the saints,
verses.kjv: That I may be delivered from them that do not believe in Judæa; and that my service which I have for Jerusalem may be accepted of the saints;
verses.bsb: Pray that I may be delivered from the unbelievers in Judea, and that my service in Jerusalem may be acceptable to the saints there,

verses.esv: so that by God's will I may come to you with joy and be refreshed in your company.
verses.kjv: That I may come unto you with joy by the will of God, and may with you be refreshed.
verses.bsb: so that by God’s will I may come to you with joy and together with you be refreshed.

verses.esv: May the God of peace be with you all. Amen.
verses.kjv: Now the God of peace be with you all. Amen.
verses.bsb: The God of peace be with all of you. Amen.

verses.esv: I commend to you our sister Phoebe, a servant of the church at Cenchreae,
verses.kjv: I COMMEND unto you Phebe our sister, which is a servant of the church which is at Cenchrea:
verses.bsb: I commend to you our sister Phoebe, a servant of the church in Cenchrea.

verses.esv: that you may welcome her in the Lord in a way worthy of the saints, and help her in whatever she may need from you, for she has been a patron of many and of myself as well.
verses.kjv: That ye receive her in the Lord, as becometh saints, and that ye assist her in whatsoever business she hath need of you: for she hath been a succourer of many, and of myself also.
verses.bsb: Welcome her in the Lord in a manner worthy of the saints, and assist her with anything she may need from you. For she has been a great help to many people, including me.

verses.esv: Greet Prisca and Aquila, my fellow workers in Christ Jesus,
verses.kjv: Greet Priscilla and Aquila my helpers in Christ Jesus:
verses.bsb: Greet Prisca and Aquila, my fellow workers in Christ Jesus,

verses.esv: who risked their necks for my life, to whom not only I give thanks but all the churches of the Gentiles give thanks as well.
verses.kjv: Who have for my life laid down their own necks: unto whom not only I give thanks, but also all the churches of the Gentiles.
verses.bsb: who have risked their lives for me. Not only I but all the churches of the Gentiles are grateful to them.

verses.esv: Greet also the church in their house. Greet my beloved Epaenetus, who was the first convert to Christ in Asia.
verses.kjv: Likewise greet the church that is in their house. Salute my wellbeloved Epænetus, who is the firstfruits of Achaia unto Christ.
verses.bsb: Greet also the church that meets at their house.  Greet my beloved Epenetus, who was the first convert to Christ in the province of Asia.

verses.esv: Greet Mary, who has worked hard for you.
verses.kjv: Greet Mary, who bestowed much labour on us.
verses.bsb: Greet Mary, who has worked very hard for you.

verses.esv: Greet Andronicus and Junia, my kinsmen and my fellow prisoners. They are well known to the apostles, and they were in Christ before me.
verses.kjv: Salute Andronicus and Junia, my kinsmen, and my fellowprisoners, who are of note among the apostles, who also were in Christ before me.
verses.bsb: Greet Andronicus and Junia, my fellow countrymen and fellow prisoners. They are outstanding among the apostles, and they were in Christ before I was.

verses.esv: Greet Ampliatus, my beloved in the Lord.
verses.kjv: Greet Amplias my beloved in the Lord.
verses.bsb: Greet Ampliatus, my beloved in the Lord.

verses.esv: Greet Urbanus, our fellow worker in Christ, and my beloved Stachys.
verses.kjv: Salute Urbane, our helper in Christ, and Stachys my beloved.
verses.bsb: Greet Urbanus, our fellow worker in Christ, and my beloved Stachys.

verses.esv: Greet Apelles, who is approved in Christ. Greet those who belong to the family of Aristobulus.
verses.kjv: Salute Apelles approved in Christ. Salute them which are of Aristobulus’ household.
verses.bsb: Greet Apelles, who is approved in Christ.  Greet those who belong to the household of Aristobulus.

verses.esv: Greet my kinsman Herodion. Greet those in the Lord who belong to the family of Narcissus.
verses.kjv: Salute Herodion my kinsman. Greet them that be of the household of Narcissus, which are in the Lord.
verses.bsb: Greet Herodion, my fellow countryman.  Greet those from the household of Narcissus who are in the Lord.

verses.esv: Greet those workers in the Lord, Tryphaena and Tryphosa. Greet the beloved Persis, who has worked hard in the Lord.
