verses.kjv: Salute Tryphena and Tryphosa, who labour in the Lord. Salute the beloved Persis, which laboured much in the Lord.
verses.bsb: Greet Tryphena and Tryphosa, women who have worked hard in the Lord.  Greet my beloved Persis, who has worked very hard in the Lord.

verses.esv: Greet Rufus, chosen in the Lord; also his mother, who has been a mother to me as well.
verses.kjv: Salute Rufus chosen in the Lord, and his mother and mine.
verses.bsb: Greet Rufus, chosen in the Lord, and his mother, who has been a mother to me as well.

verses.esv: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers who are with them.
verses.kjv: Salute Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermas, Patrobas, Hermes, and the brethren which are with them.
verses.bsb: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers with them.

verses.esv: Greet Philologus, Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints who are with them.
verses.kjv: Salute Philologus, and Julia, Nereus, and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints which are with them.
verses.bsb: Greet Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas and all the saints with them.

verses.esv: Greet one another with a holy kiss. All the churches of Christ greet you.
verses.kjv: Salute one another with an holy kiss. The churches of Christ salute you.
verses.bsb: Greet one another with a holy kiss.  All the churches of Christ send you greetings.

verses.esv: I appeal to you, brothers, to watch out for those who cause divisions and create obstacles contrary to the doctrine that you have been taught; avoid them.
verses.kjv: Now I beseech you, brethren, mark them which cause divisions and offences contrary to the doctrine which ye have learned; and avoid them.
verses.bsb: Now I urge you, brothers, to watch out for those who create divisions and obstacles that are contrary to the teaching you have learned. Turn away from them.

verses.esv: For such persons do not serve our Lord Christ, but their own appetites, and by smooth talk and flattery they deceive the hearts of the naive.
verses.kjv: For they that are such serve not our Lord Jesus Christ, but their own belly; and by good words and fair speeches deceive the hearts of the simple.
verses.bsb: For such people are not serving our Lord Christ, but their own appetites. By smooth talk and flattery they deceive the hearts of the naive.

verses.esv: For your obedience is known to all, so that I rejoice over you, but I want you to be wise as to what is good and innocent as to what is evil.
verses.kjv: For your obedience is come abroad unto all men. I am glad therefore on your behalf: but yet I would have you wise unto that which is good, and simple concerning evil.
verses.bsb: Everyone has heard about your obedience, so I rejoice over you. But I want you to be wise about what is good and innocent about what is evil.

verses.esv: The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
verses.kjv: And the God of peace shall bruise Satan under your feet shortly. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.
verses.bsb: The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.

verses.esv: Timothy, my fellow worker, greets you; so do Lucius and Jason and Sosipater, my kinsmen.
verses.kjv: Timotheus my workfellow, and Lucius, and Jason, and Sosipater, my kinsmen, salute you.
verses.bsb: Timothy, my fellow worker, sends you greetings, as do Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater, my fellow countrymen.

verses.esv: I Tertius, who wrote this letter, greet you in the Lord.
verses.kjv: I Tertius, who wrote this epistle, salute you in the Lord.
verses.bsb: I, Tertius, who wrote down this letter, greet you in the Lord.

verses.esv: Gaius, who is host to me and to the whole church, greets you. Erastus, the city treasurer, and our brother Quartus, greet you.
verses.kjv: Gaius mine host, and of the whole church, saluteth you. Erastus the chamberlain of the city saluteth you, and Quartus a brother.
verses.bsb: Gaius, who has hosted me and all the church, sends you greetings.  Erastus, the city treasurer, sends you greetings, as does our brother Quartus.

verses.esv: Now to him who is able to strengthen you according to my gospel and the preaching of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the mystery that was kept secret for long ages
verses.kjv: Now to him that is of power to stablish you according to my gospel, and the preaching of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the mystery, which was kept secret since the world began,
verses.bsb: Now to Him who is able to strengthen you by my gospel and by the proclamation of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the mystery concealed for ages past

verses.esv: but has now been disclosed and through the prophetic writings has been made known to all nations, according to the command of the eternal God, to bring about the obedience of faith—
verses.kjv: But now is made manifest, and by the scriptures of the prophets, according to the commandment of the everlasting God, made known to all nations for the obedience of faith:
verses.bsb: but now revealed and made known through the writings of the prophets by the command of the eternal God, in order to lead all nations to the obedience that comes from faith—

verses.esv: to the only wise God be glory forevermore through Jesus Christ! Amen.
verses.kjv: To God only wise, be glory through Jesus Christ for ever. Amen. ¶ Written to the Romans from Corinthus, and sent by Phebe servant of the church at Cenchrea. THE
verses.bsb: to the only wise God be glory forever through Jesus Christ! Amen.
